response:
Emily collected 24 blueberries which served as the starting point for the group's collection. David gathered 15 more than Emily meaning he collected 39 blueberries in total. His collection was also three times as many as Sophia's which means Sophia collected 13 blueberries. The total number of blueberries they had together was 24 plus 39 plus 13 which equals 76 blueberries.

***
They used their blueberries to make muffins and each batch required 9 blueberries. With 76 blueberries available they could make several batches of muffins. Dividing 76 by 9 shows that they could make 8 full batches with 4 blueberries left over. Each batch was sold for 5 dollars so the total money earned from the batches was 8 multiplied by 5.

***
The leftover blueberries were not enough to make another batch so they were not used for selling. The group focused on maximizing the number of batches they could produce. Every batch contributed to their earnings and the process was efficient with no waste of resources. The money from the muffins was their main source of income from the blueberries.

***
The total amount they made from the blueberries was 40 dollars. This came from selling 8 batches at 5 dollars each. The experience taught them about teamwork and resource management. Their efforts reflected the spirit of youth.
